text.title: Design Doc Structure Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Design Doc Structure. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every design doc contains five sections in order: Context, Proposed Design, Alternatives Considered, Risks, Rollout.
rule.rationale: Predictable order cuts reviewer scan time in half.

rule.statement: Sections may be marked N/A only with a one-line justification; blank sections are rejected.
rule.rationale: Blank sections hide skipped work; explicit N/A signals an intentional decision.

rule.statement: Architectural decisions cite ADR numbers (AD-X); free-floating decisions are rejected.
rule.rationale: ADR citations make the design doc auditable against the historical record.

rule.statement: Every risk lists at least one mitigation OR explicit accept-risk with owner.
rule.rationale: Risks without mitigations decay into anxiety; force the decision.

rule.statement: Rollout section specifies rollback steps OR explicit 'no rollback' with rationale.
rule.rationale: No-rollback designs are valid for some changes but must be intentional.

rule.statement: Design doc links to the upstream spec.md by path; orphan design docs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without the spec link, the design has no source-of-truth anchor.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-design-doc-structure.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
